info.title: Blueticks API
info.description: Public API for Blueticks WhatsApp automation.

tags.name: Account
tags.description: Account metadata for the authenticated API key.

tags.name: Audiences
tags.description: Reusable contact lists used as targets for messages and campaigns.

tags.name: Campaigns
tags.description: Schedule audiences for paced bulk delivery with pause/resume/cancel controls.

tags.name: Chats
tags.description: Read WhatsApp chats and their messages on the connected engine.

tags.name: Contacts
tags.description: Read WhatsApp contacts known to the connected engine.

tags.name: Engines
tags.description: Inspect or control the WhatsApp engine(s) connected to the workspace.

tags.name: Groups
tags.description: Create and manage WhatsApp groups via the connected engine.

tags.name: Newsletters
tags.description: Create and inspect WhatsApp newsletters (channels) via the connected engine.

tags.name: Ping
tags.description: Health/identity probe — verifies the API key and echoes its scopes.

tags.name: Scheduled Messages
tags.description: Send or schedule WhatsApp messages via the user-messages queue. Each request creates a queued send — set `send_at` (RFC 3339) to defer delivery, omit it to send immediately. List, retrieve, edit, and cancel queued sends.

tags.name: Webhooks
tags.description: Register URLs to receive signed HTTPS POSTs on Blueticks events.

get.description: Probe the API: returns the account ID, key prefix, and granted scopes for the authenticated API key. Useful as a connection test and to inspect what an integration is allowed to do. No scope required.

account_id.description: The account ID the API key belongs to.

key_prefix.description: Public prefix of the API key (first 8 chars, never the full secret).

scopes.description: Scopes granted to this API key.

422.description: Validation failed — see `error.details` for per-field issues.

get.description: List messages in the user-messages queue (all sources: API, dashboard, extension), newest first (cursor-paginated). Optionally filter by `chat_id` and/or lifecycle `status`.

parameters.name: limit
parameters.description: Page size (1-200, default 50).

parameters.name: cursor
parameters.description: Opaque cursor from a previous response's `next_cursor`.

parameters.name: chat_id
parameters.description: Filter to messages addressed to this WhatsApp JID. Matches the recipient stored on the queued/scheduled doc — for sends to a phone number, the JID is `<phone>@c.us`.

parameters.name: status
parameters.description: Filter by lifecycle status. `scheduled` returns pending messages whose `send_at` is in the future; `queued` returns pending messages ready for immediate dispatch; `sending`/`delivered`/`read`/`failed` reflect post-dispatch states. Omit to return all statuses.

parameters.name: q
parameters.description: Substring match over message body (case-insensitive).

400.description: Bad request — invalid params or body.

403.description: API key lacks the required scope.

422.description: Validation failed — see `error.details` for per-field issues.

429.description: Rate limit exceeded — back off and retry.

post.description:
Send a message via WhatsApp. The body is a discriminated union — set the `type` field to one of `text`, `media`, or `poll`.

**Variants:**

- `type: "text"` — required `text` (1–4096 chars). Example body:
  ```json
  {"type":"text","to":"+15551234567","text":"hello"}
  ```
- `type: "media"` — required `media.url` (HTTPS). Optional `media.kind` (`image` · `video` · `audio` · `document` · `sticker` · `voice`; auto-detected when omitted), `media.caption`, `media.filename`. Example:
  ```json
  {"type":"media","to":"+15551234567","media":{"url":"https://cdn.example.com/x.pdf","kind":"document","filename":"receipt.pdf"}}
  ```
- `type: "poll"` — required `poll.question` and `poll.options` (2–12 items). Optional `poll.allow_multiple` (default false). Example:
  ```json
  {"type":"poll","to":"+15551234567","poll":{"question":"Pizza?","options":["Yes","No"]}}
  ```

All variants accept optional `send_at` (ISO 8601 ≥10s future, ≤365d), `from` (E.164 sender for multi-session workspaces), and `reply_to` (wire `key` of a prior message to quote).

See [Sending messages](/docs/messages) for runnable Python / Node / PHP / cURL examples per variant.

parameters.name: Idempotency-Key
parameters.description: Optional key for safe retries. Identical bodies with the same key return the original response; differing bodies return 409.

patch.description: Edit a previously-queued message that has not dispatched yet. Accepts a subset of `text`, `media_url`, `media_caption`, `send_at` — at least one is required. Returns 400 once the message has advanced past the editable window (status not in `pending`/`sending`).

text.description: New text body. Replaces the previous text/caption.

media_url.description: Replace the attached media URL (https only).

media_caption.description: Replace the media's caption. When set alongside `text`, `text` wins.

send_at.description: Reschedule. ISO 8601 datetime (UTC) in the future.

post.description: Create a new audience. Returns the audience with `contact_count: 0`. Requires `audiences:write`.

to.description: Recipient. Either +E164 phone (e.g. +14155551234) or a WhatsApp JID (e.g. 14155551234@c.us). Stored as +E164.

variables.description:
Per-contact substitution values used by campaign templates.
Each key becomes a token usable as `{key}` in a campaign `text`. Lookup is case-insensitive (e.g. `{firstName}` resolves `firstname`).
Built-in tokens always available regardless of variables: `{whatsappname}`, `{displayname}`, `{phone}`, `{mobile}`. Setting a variable with the same name overrides the built-in.
Storage: keys are persisted as text-cell column titles on the audience board, so values flow back via audiences.get under the same names.
Limits: at most 32 keys per contact; values up to 1024 chars; keys must match /^[A-Za-z_][A-Za-z0-9_]{0,31}$/.

post.description: Schedule a new bulk-message campaign. Returns the campaign in `pending` state. Requires `campaigns:write`.

audience_id.description: ID of the audience to target. Each audience contact contributes one outgoing message.

text.description:
Message body. Supports per-contact substitution using single-brace tokens like `{firstName}` or `{product}`.
Resolution rules (case-insensitive, whitespace stripped):
  - Built-ins always available: `{whatsappname}` (WA profile name), `{displayname}` (firstname+lastname fallback), `{phone}` / `{mobile}`.
  - Custom variables: every property attached to a contact via audiences.append_contacts becomes a token of the same name.
  - Unresolved tokens are left as literal `{token}` in the sent message.
Templates use SINGLE braces `{var}` — `{{var}}` is treated as literal text.

media_url.description: Public https URL of an image/video/document to attach.

media_caption.description: Caption for the media attachment. Requires media_url.

from.description: Sender phone in +E164. When omitted, the workspace default sender is used.

on_missing_variable.description:
Behavior when a template references a variable that is not present on a contact:
  - `fail` (default): reject the request at create time; no messages are sent. Returns 400 with `data.examples` listing offending contacts.
  - `skip`: accept the campaign; contacts missing variables receive the message with literal `{token}` left in place.

get.description: List engines connected to this workspace. Returns an array (currently up to one entry, but the response shape leaves room for multi-engine workspaces). When no engine is connected, `data` is empty and `message` explains why — this is not an error.

connected.description: Whether this engine is currently online and serving requests.

state.description: Engine-side connection state (e.g. CONNECTED, OPENING, PAIRING). Null when unknown.

stream.description: Underlying WA stream state. Null when unknown.

has_synced.description: Whether the engine has completed its initial history sync.

data.description: Engines connected to this workspace. Empty when no engine is paired.

message.description: Human-readable note (e.g. why the array is empty). Present only when useful.

get.description: Cursor-paginated list of messages in a chat. Supports free-text search (`query`), date range (`since`/`until`), and message-kind filtering (`message_types`). Requires `chats:read`.

parameters.name: order
parameters.description: Result ordering. asc = oldest-first; desc = newest-first (default).

parameters.name: mode
parameters.description: Deprecated alias — prefer `order` (asc/desc). history=asc, latest=desc.

parameters.name: q
parameters.description: Free-text search across message bodies and captions.

parameters.name: query
parameters.description: Deprecated alias for `q` — prefer `q`. Will be removed in v2.

parameters.name: limit
parameters.description: Max messages to return.

parameters.name: cursor
parameters.description: Pagination cursor from a previous response.

parameters.name: since
parameters.description: Lower bound (ISO 8601). Only messages with timestamp >= since.

parameters.name: until
parameters.description: Upper bound (ISO 8601). Only messages with timestamp <= until.

parameters.name: message_types
parameters.description: Filter to specific message kinds. Comma-separated or repeated. Useful for "all PDFs" (`document`) or "images today" (`image`). System events (gp2/revoked/newsletter_notification) are excluded by default unless explicitly listed here.

parameters.name: load_from_phone_if_needed
parameters.description: When the local store is exhausted, fetch older messages from the phone (chrome only).

parameters.name: include_media_content
parameters.description: Include inline media payload on rows (heavy; default false — use /messages/{key}/media).

400.description: Bad request — invalid params or body.

422.description: Validation failed — see `error.details` for per-field issues.

get.description:
Download the media attached to a WhatsApp message (image, video, document, audio).
Returns either a hosted URL (`url`) or inline `data_base64`, plus mimetype + filename.

CAVEAT: for own-sent newsletter media (messages you sent to a `@newsletter` chat), the bytes returned are a WA-generated preview JPEG (~7KB) rather than the original you uploaded — WA releases the original blob from memory immediately after the unencrypted upload. The response will include `original_quality:false` when this fallback is in effect; the caller can warn the user or decide to retry from a different source.

Requires `chats:read`.

original_quality.description: Set to false when the bytes returned are NOT the original sender uploaded — i.e. WA released the original from memory and we synthesized from a smaller WA-generated preview (#113). Currently only happens for own-sent newsletter media (messages you sent to a @newsletter chat). When absent or true, the bytes are the genuine original. Consumers can use this to warn users / decide whether to retry from a different source.

media_unavailable.description: Set when the bytes could not be downloaded or never existed. "expired" = WA aged the file out of CDN retention (typically older than ~30 days for documents) and there is nothing to retry. "fetching" = WA is mid-download from its CDN — transient, the server already retried for ~10s; calling again in a few seconds usually resolves to bytes. "awaiting_sender" = WA's CDN does not have the file but has asked the sender's device to reupload it. The reupload only completes when the sender's WhatsApp client is open and online; closed/backgrounded apps block it indefinitely. Tell the user to ask the sender to open WhatsApp; do not poll. "error" = unexpected failure; "no_media" = the message is text-only / revoked / location / vcard, i.e. it never carried media — caller should not have invoked this tool on this messageKey. Absent when bytes were not needed or when the fetch succeeded.

400.description: Bad request — invalid params or body.

403.description: API key lacks the required scope.

422.description: Validation failed — see `error.details` for per-field issues.

429.description: Rate limit exceeded — back off and retry.

timezone.description: IANA timezone (e.g. "America/New_York"), or null if unset.

to.description: Recipient. Either +E164 phone (e.g. +14155551234) or a WhatsApp JID (e.g. 14155551234@c.us). Stored as +E164.

variables.description:
Per-contact substitution values used by campaign templates.
Each key becomes a token usable as `{key}` in a campaign `text`. Lookup is case-insensitive (e.g. `{firstName}` resolves `firstname`).
Built-in tokens always available regardless of variables: `{whatsappname}`, `{displayname}`, `{phone}`, `{mobile}`. Setting a variable with the same name overrides the built-in.
Storage: keys are persisted as text-cell column titles on the audience board, so values flow back via audiences.get under the same names.
Limits: at most 32 keys per contact; values up to 1024 chars; keys must match /^[A-Za-z_][A-Za-z0-9_]{0,31}$/.

to.description: Recipient. Either +E164 phone (e.g. +14155551234) or a WhatsApp JID (e.g. 14155551234@c.us). Stored as +E164.

audience_id.description: ID of the audience to target. Each audience contact contributes one outgoing message.

text.description:
Message body. Supports per-contact substitution using single-brace tokens like `{firstName}` or `{product}`.
Resolution rules (case-insensitive, whitespace stripped):
  - Built-ins always available: `{whatsappname}` (WA profile name), `{displayname}` (firstname+lastname fallback), `{phone}` / `{mobile}`.
  - Custom variables: every property attached to a contact via audiences.append_contacts becomes a token of the same name.
  - Unresolved tokens are left as literal `{token}` in the sent message.
Templates use SINGLE braces `{var}` — `{{var}}` is treated as literal text.

media_url.description: Public https URL of an image/video/document to attach.

media_caption.description: Caption for the media attachment. Requires media_url.

from.description: Sender phone in +E164. When omitted, the workspace default sender is used.

on_missing_variable.description:
Behavior when a template references a variable that is not present on a contact:
  - `fail` (default): reject the request at create time; no messages are sent. Returns 400 with `data.examples` listing offending contacts.
  - `skip`: accept the campaign; contacts missing variables receive the message with literal `{token}` left in place.

q.description: Case-insensitive substring match against chat name / phone.

query.description: Deprecated alias for `q` — prefer `q`. Will be removed in v2.

cursor.description: Pagination cursor from a previous response.

include_last_message.description: Include the last message snippet on each chat row.

include_without_name.description: Include chats with no resolved name (default: skipped).

cursor.description: Pagination cursor from a previous response.

offset.description: Explicit offset alternative to `cursor`. Cursor wins when both are set.

order.description: Result ordering. asc = oldest-first; desc = newest-first (default).

q.description: Free-text search across message bodies and captions.

query.description: Deprecated alias for `q` — prefer `q`. Will be removed in v2.

cursor.description: Pagination cursor from a previous response.

since.description: Lower bound (ISO 8601). Only messages with timestamp >= since.

until.description: Upper bound (ISO 8601). Only messages with timestamp <= until.

message_types.description: Filter to specific message kinds. Comma-separated or repeated. Useful for "all PDFs" (`document`) or "images today" (`image`). System events (gp2/revoked/newsletter_notification) are excluded by default unless explicitly listed here.

load_from_phone_if_needed.description: When the local store is exhausted, fetch older messages from the phone (chrome only).

include_media_content.description: Include inline media payload on rows (heavy; default false — use /messages/{key}/media).

unmute_at.description: ISO 8601 timestamp at which the chat auto-unmutes. Omit for "forever".

label_ids.description: Full replacement set of label ids for this chat.

note.description: Free-text note. Empty string clears the note.

result.description: `true` when WAWeb accepted the mutation; `false` when WAWeb refused or the feature is unavailable on this account.

error.description: Set when `result === false`. The literal `wa_business_required` signals the connected account is not a WhatsApp Business account; any other value is the underlying engine diagnostic.

original_quality.description: Set to false when the bytes returned are NOT the original sender uploaded — i.e. WA released the original from memory and we synthesized from a smaller WA-generated preview (#113). Currently only happens for own-sent newsletter media (messages you sent to a @newsletter chat). When absent or true, the bytes are the genuine original. Consumers can use this to warn users / decide whether to retry from a different source.

media_unavailable.description: Set when the bytes could not be downloaded or never existed. "expired" = WA aged the file out of CDN retention (typically older than ~30 days for documents) and there is nothing to retry. "fetching" = WA is mid-download from its CDN — transient, the server already retried for ~10s; calling again in a few seconds usually resolves to bytes. "awaiting_sender" = WA's CDN does not have the file but has asked the sender's device to reupload it. The reupload only completes when the sender's WhatsApp client is open and online; closed/backgrounded apps block it indefinitely. Tell the user to ask the sender to open WhatsApp; do not poll. "error" = unexpected failure; "no_media" = the message is text-only / revoked / location / vcard, i.e. it never carried media — caller should not have invoked this tool on this messageKey. Absent when bytes were not needed or when the fetch succeeded.

connected.description: Whether this engine is currently online and serving requests.

state.description: Engine-side connection state (e.g. CONNECTED, OPENING, PAIRING). Null when unknown.

stream.description: Underlying WA stream state. Null when unknown.

has_synced.description: Whether the engine has completed its initial history sync.

connected.description: Whether this engine is currently online and serving requests.

state.description: Engine-side connection state (e.g. CONNECTED, OPENING, PAIRING). Null when unknown.

stream.description: Underlying WA stream state. Null when unknown.

has_synced.description: Whether the engine has completed its initial history sync.

data.description: Engines connected to this workspace. Empty when no engine is paired.

message.description: Human-readable note (e.g. why the array is empty). Present only when useful.

cursor.description: Opaque cursor returned by a prior call. Omit for the first page.

q.description: Case-insensitive substring match against the group name.

path.description: Dotted path to the offending field within the request body.

message.description: Human-readable description of what went wrong.

request_id.description: Server-assigned request id; pass this when filing support tickets so engineering can trace the failure.

path.description: Dotted path to the offending field within the request body.

details.description: Per-field validation issues. Present on 400/422 when the request body failed Zod parsing.

ErrorEnvelope.description: Standard error response envelope. Every 4xx/5xx response from the v1 API carries this exact shape.

message.description: Human-readable description of what went wrong.

request_id.description: Server-assigned request id; pass this when filing support tickets so engineering can trace the failure.

path.description: Dotted path to the offending field within the request body.

details.description: Per-field validation issues. Present on 400/422 when the request body failed Zod parsing.

Error.description: Standard error response envelope. Every 4xx/5xx response from the v1 API carries this exact shape.

data.description: Page of items; the item shape is endpoint-specific.

has_more.description: `true` when more items exist past this page; use `next_cursor` to fetch them.

next_cursor.description: Opaque cursor for the next page, or `null` when `has_more` is `false`.

PaginatedResponse.description: Cursor-paginated response envelope. Every v1 list endpoint returns this shape.

cursor.description: Opaque cursor returned by a prior call. Omit for the first page.

name.description: Display name of the newsletter / WhatsApp channel.

owner.description: Owner JID, if returned by the engine.

account_id.description: The account ID the API key belongs to.

key_prefix.description: Public prefix of the API key (first 8 chars, never the full secret).

scopes.description: Scopes granted to this API key.

cursor.description: Opaque cursor from a previous response's `next_cursor`.

chat_id.description: Filter to messages addressed to this WhatsApp JID. Matches the recipient stored on the queued/scheduled doc — for sends to a phone number, the JID is `<phone>@c.us`.

status.description: Filter by lifecycle status. `scheduled` returns pending messages whose `send_at` is in the future; `queued` returns pending messages ready for immediate dispatch; `sending`/`delivered`/`read`/`failed` reflect post-dispatch states. Omit to return all statuses.

text.description: New text body. Replaces the previous text/caption.

media_url.description: Replace the attached media URL (https only).

media_caption.description: Replace the media's caption. When set alongside `text`, `text` wins.

send_at.description: Reschedule. ISO 8601 datetime (UTC) in the future.

url.description: Bare URL shortcut. Sends the URL as the body and force-enables link preview.

link_preview.description: Controls the rich-preview card rendered under a URL in the text. true (default) → auto-fetch when a URL is present. false → never attach a preview. object → use the caller-supplied preview verbatim.

reply_to.description: Wire `key` of a prior message to quote-reply (from MessageResponse.key).

mentions.description: JIDs to mention. Highlights/notifies the listed users; `displays` is the rendered name list.

data_base64.description: Raw media bytes, base64-encoded. Alternative to `url`.

kind.description: Media kind. Optional — falls back to engine auto-detection from URL/Content-Type.

reply_to.description: Wire `key` of a prior message to quote-reply (from MessageResponse.key).

mentions.description: JIDs to mention. Highlights/notifies the listed users; `displays` is the rendered name list.

reply_to.description: Wire `key` of a prior message to quote-reply (from MessageResponse.key).

mentions.description: JIDs to mention. Highlights/notifies the listed users; `displays` is the rendered name list.
